name: go_mod_graph
description: Analyze Go module registration patterns in a project. Finds all module.Register() and module.RegisterEndpoint() calls with their package locations, module names, and surrounding context. Can also show interface definitions and implementation registrations.
when_to_use: Use when analyzing Go project structure, especially to understand the module/plugin registration system. Replaces 5-10+ separate grep/cat/source_reader calls (e.g. separate searches for module.Register, module.RegisterEndpoint, and interface definitions). Best used after explore_project to understand the project language.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Go project (e.g. /app)

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: 'all' (register + endpoints + interfaces, default), 'register' (module.Register only), 'endpoints' (module.RegisterEndpoint only), 'interfaces' (type interface definitions), 'graph' (all with dependency mapping)

parameters.name: max_matches
parameters.description: Maximum number of matches to show per category (default: 80)

examples.expected: All module.Register(), module.RegisterEndpoint() calls, and interface definitions in the project.
Complete module graph with registered modules, endpoints, and interfaces they implement.
Up to 50 interface definitions in the project.

cost_saving_rationale: Combines 5-10 separate grep calls (module.Register, module.RegisterEndpoint, interface definitions) and multiple cat/source_reader calls to read individual registration files into a single script call. Reduces round trips by showing all module registration patterns in one structured output. The 'graph' action provides a complete module dependency view that previously required manually correlating multiple grep outputs.
